title: Listed markets can rewrite their own `version` in the Comptroller without admin approval
reason: `updateBTokenVersion` is callable only by the listed BToken itself and only changes the controller-side enter/exit hook selection. In this snapshot that is not a standalone user-triggerable exploit; it depends on a trusted market contract or its upgrade/admin path already acting maliciously.

title: Flash Loan Missing Liquidity and Collateral Checks
reason: Flash loans are intentionally uncollateralized, and the provided code does not include a flash-loan implementation showing an unpaid-loan path. The Comptroller pause hook alone is not evidence of a drain.

title: Soft-Delisted Markets Still Allow Critical Operations
reason: Soft delisting intentionally preserves unwind flows. `mintAllowed` and `borrowAllowed` require `isMarketListed`, so new supply/borrows are blocked; allowing redeem/repay/liquidation on soft-delisted markets is expected behavior, not a bug.

title: Unchecked Liquidation Incentive Allows Zero Value
reason: This is an admin-only parameter choice, not a permissionless flaw. Governance can always make liquidation uneconomic by misconfiguring incentives.

title: Unbounded Credit Limits Allow Infinite Borrowing
reason: Unsecured credit lines are an explicit privileged feature here. The meaningful bug is the inability to resolve debt after reducing a still-positive limit, which is captured in F-003.

title: Market Delisting Doesn't Check Outstanding Borrows
reason: Merged into F-001. The reportable issue is specifically the hard-delist path, which both hides live debt from liquidity checks and blocks normal resolution.

title: Guardian Can Permanently Pause Markets Without Oversight
reason: Guardian/admin pause powers are explicit trusted-role emergency controls. This is governance/operational risk, not a protocol bug.

title: No Price Freshness Validation in Oracle
reason: Price freshness is delegated to the oracle implementation. No stale-price exploit is provable from the Comptroller code alone.

title: Zero Supply/Borrow Caps Lock Markets Permanently
reason: Zero caps are documented admin/guardian configuration semantics, not an unintended code defect.

title: No Protection Against Sandwich Attacks in Liquidation
reason: This is a generic MEV concern affecting public liquidations, not a protocol-specific vulnerability in the audited code.

title: Interest Accrual Can Be Delayed Indefinitely
reason: Lazy interest accrual on interaction is standard lending-market behavior and not, by itself, a vulnerability.

title: Missing Access Control on Credit Limit Manager
reason: Access control exists: only admin sets `creditLimitManager`, and only admin or that manager can change limits. The candidate describes trusted-role abuse, not missing access control.

title: RedeemAllowed Allows Redemption from Delisted Markets Without Liquidity Check
reason: If an account is not in the market, that asset is not being used as collateral, so bypassing the liquidity check is intentional. Market cash sufficiency is enforced on the BToken side, not here.
